content.claim: Cross-country evidence using differences-in-differences analysis on international assessments finds that early tracking at ages 10–12 increases educational inequality between students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Effect sizes not specified in available notes
Study uses cross-national test data which may not capture all dimensions of educational opportunity
Findings reflect systems-level patterns and may not isolate 6/8-year gimnázium effect specifically
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
